# Security Checklist: PR Blast-Radius Review Action

**Purpose**: Validate requirements quality for fork trust boundaries, token permissions, secret handling, cache trust, comment ownership, and narrative suppression.
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md), [plan.md](../plan.md), [data-model.md](../data-model.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are same-repository, fork-like, read-only-token, and secret-unavailable trust states all represented in requirements? [Completeness, Spec FR-030-FR-033, Data Model Trust Boundary]
- [x] CHK002 Are least-privilege token requirements documented for deterministic analysis and comment delivery separately? [Completeness, Spec FR-031]
- [x] CHK003 Are secret-backed narrative requirements defined separately from deterministic analysis requirements? [Completeness, Spec FR-017, FR-033, FR-046-FR-047]
- [x] CHK004 Are cache identity and restored-path trust requirements defined before cache use? [Completeness, Spec FR-027-FR-029]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is `pull_request_target` avoidance for untrusted code stated clearly enough to guide workflow design? [Clarity, Spec FR-030]
- [x] CHK006 Is comment ownership scoped to stable hidden markers so unrelated comments are protected? [Clarity, Spec FR-004-FR-005, FR-034-FR-036]
- [x] CHK007 Is narrative authority limited to prose-only output with no machine-consumed status influence? [Clarity, Spec FR-018, FR-046-FR-047]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Do fork-support requirements align with summary/artifact fallback and no-secret narrative suppression? [Consistency, Spec US2, FR-006, FR-017, FR-032-FR-033]
- [x] CHK009 Do cache validation requirements align with local-first/privacy constraints and avoid trusting unverified restored state? [Consistency, Spec FR-013-FR-015, FR-027-FR-029]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK010 Are permission-denied, deleted-comment, duplicate-comment, and rerun-race scenarios all represented as requirement cases? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, FR-034-FR-039]
- [x] CHK011 Are untrusted narrative and trusted narrative paths both covered by measurable success criteria? [Coverage, Spec SC-012, SC-015]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK012 Are GitHub token/fork behavior assumptions documented in research and connected to requirements? [Dependency, Plan Research, Spec FR-031-FR-032]
